ocr.text: Variable  SCA2 patients(N = 48)  Preclinical carriers(N = 25)  Healthy controls(N = 24)
Gender (female/male)  19/29  16/9  15/9
Age at onset (years)  30.06 ± 9.06(9–60)  NA  NA
Disease duration (years)  11.71 ± 6.08(3–29)  NA  NA
Expanded CAG repeat (units)  39.81 ± 2.92(35–47)  35.76 ± 1.90(32–39)  NA
Variables  Group 1 n (%)  Group 2 n (%)  p
Number of bypass grafts  2.9 ± 0.6  2.9 ± 0.8  0.91
Time of cross clamping (min)  32.6 ± 9.4  31.2 ± 6.9  0.78
Carotid clamping time (min)  9.8 ± 2.7  9.7 ± 3.1  0.91
In a study involving SCA2 patients (N = 48), preclinical carriers (N = 25), and healthy controls (N = 24), gender distribution
was 19/29, 16/9, and 15/9 respectively. The mean age for SCA2 patients was 41.35 ± 9.79 (range 18–69), for preclinical carriers
it was 36.88 ± 9.14 (24–61), and for healthy controls it was 41.13 ± 11.05 (20–63). Age at onset for SCA2 patients averaged
30.06 ± 9.06 (9–60), while data for preclinical carriers and controls was not available. The disease duration for SCA2 patients
was 11.71 ± 6.08 (3–29). SARA scores were 15.81 ± 5.59 (6–30) for SCA2 patients, 0.76 ± 0.66 (0–2) for preclinical carriers,
and 0 for healthy controls. The unexpanded CAG repeat was 22.08 ± 0.92 (19–27) for SCA2 patients, 22.84 ± 1.93 (22–29) for
preclinical carriers, and 22.54 ± 1.76 (20–30) for controls, while expanded CAG repeats were 39.81 ± 2.92 (35–47) for SCA2
patients and 35.76 ± 1.90 (32–39) for preclinical carriers with no data for controls. In a separate analysis, Group 1 exhibited 2.9
± 0.6 bypass grafts and Group 2 showed 2.9 ± 0.8 (p = 0.91), with CPB time recorded at 72.3 ± 21.9 minutes for Group 1 and
59.6 ± 20.8 minutes for Group 2 (p = 0.03). Time of cross clamping was 32.6 ± 9.4 minutes for Group 1 and 31.2 ± 6.9 minutes
for Group 2 (p = 0.78), while carotid clamping time was 9.8 ± 2.7 minutes for Group 1 and 9.7 ± 3.1 minutes for Group 2 (p =
0.91). Hypothermia durations were 30.3 ± 1.3 minutes for Group 1 and 35.8 ± 0.7 minutes for Group 2 (p = 0.001), and left
CEA was performed in 17 (50%) of Group 1 and 14 (61%) of Group 2 (p = 0.41).
